T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts.

T1078.004.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
T1078.004.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse cloud credentials for initial access or privilege escalation.

T1098.name: Account Manipulation
T1098.description: Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain and/or elevate access.

T1098.003.name: Account Manipulation: Additional Cloud Roles
T1098.003.description: Adversaries may add additional roles to cloud accounts to maintain persistent access.

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.description: Adversaries may exploit a public-facing application to gain initial access.

T1199.name: Trusted Relationship
T1199.description: Adversaries may breach trusted third-party relationships to gain initial access.

T1021.name: Remote Services
T1021.description: Adversaries may use remote services to move laterally within the network.

T1040.name: Network Sniffing
T1040.description: Adversaries may sniff network traffic to capture credentials or data.

T1213.002.name: Data from Information Repositories: SharePoint
T1213.002.description: Adversaries may collect data from SharePoint repositories.

T1525.name: Implant Internal Image
T1525.description: Adversaries may implant container images to establish persistence.

T1530.name: Data from Cloud Storage
T1530.description: Adversaries may access data from improperly secured cloud storage.

T1537.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
T1537.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data to a cloud account they control.

T1550.001.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
T1550.001.description: Adversaries may use application access tokens to bypass authentication.

T1552.001.name: Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
T1552.001.description: Adversaries may search for credentials stored in files.

T1610.name: Deploy Container
T1610.description: Adversaries may deploy containers to execute malicious workloads.
